claimText: The Arabian Sea Mini Warm Pool is located in the southeastern Arabian Sea near the Kerala coast, characterized by sea surface temperatures exceeding 28.5 degrees Celsius.
evidenceExcerpt: Located in the southeastern Arabian Sea near the Kerala coast with SST exceeding 28.5 degrees Celsius.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical location and temperature characteristics of the Mini Warm Pool.

claimText: The Arabian Sea Mini Warm Pool forms annually during April and May, just before the onset of the Indian Summer Monsoon.
evidenceExcerpt: Forms annually during April and May just before the Indian Summer Monsoon.
examUse: Statement-based question on the seasonal timing of the Mini Warm Pool's formation.
distractorHooks: Forms after the Indian Summer Monsoon

claimText: The Arabian Sea Mini Warm Pool functions as a self-correcting mechanism that assists the Indian Summer Monsoon in recovering from prior El Nino disruptions.
evidenceExcerpt: Acts as a self-correcting mechanism helping the monsoon recover from previous El Nino disruptions.
examUse: Statement-based question on the climatic role and impact of the Mini Warm Pool on the Indian monsoon.
distractorHooks: Helps recover from La Nina disruptions
